title: Shoyu Ramen
description: A classic Japanese Shoyu Ramen from Shikoku, known for its rich and savory flavor, velvety texture, and aromatic golden hue. This low-glycemic-friendly dish is perfect for a midnight craving.
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add sliced ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add minced garlic and cook for another 1-2 minutes, until golden brown.
Pour in pork bone broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in soy sauce and golden caster sugar.
Cook egg noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a small pan, heat a tablespoon of vegetable oil over medium heat.
Flamb0 the oil until slightly caramelized.
Add chopped scallions and cook until slightly wilted.
To assemble the ramen, place cooked noodles in a bowl and ladle hot broth over the top.
Top with chopped bok choy, flamb0 scallions, and a soft-boiled egg (optional).
